floors.subtitle: Drinks and DJ selections free entry

detail.description:
Bring Your Ass
has been a party series inviting ambassadors of
house music
, guests as Aaron-Carl, Whodat, Marcellus Pittman, and Kyle Hall when he was just 18 years old, way back when internet was not taking that much space. Open Ground is giving the spirit of those parties a new home to continue a deep house journey on a soundsystem comparable in it's intensity to the high technical standard originaly set on US American deep house dancefloors like
The Shelter
.

Let's transform.

slots.bio: Berlin-based DJ and radio host Elke radiates high energy – whether through playful electro, garage, and jungle or via percussive techno, bassy rollers, and stone-cold drumfunk.
slots.name: Elke

slots.bio: The first superstar produced by the breakbeat jungle movement, Goldie popularized drum’n’bass as a form of musical expression just as relevant for living-room contemplation as techno had become by the early ’90s. Goldie became one of the first personalities in British dance music, his gold teeth and b-boy attitude placing him leagues away from the faceless bedroom boffins who had become the norm in intelligent dance music.
slots.name: Goldie

slots.bio: A true veteran and leading tastemaker in Doc Scott has been an essential and pivotal figure in D&B scene since before its inception and continues to push things forward with his future beats ethos. Whilst well known for shaping Drum & Bass, he’s equally passionate about links between Detroit Techno and Drum n Bass It all came from the same DNA originally and parallels and bridges between the two worlds still very much exist today.
slots.name: Doc Scott

floors.name: ANNEX

slots.bio: Viola Klein’s records are supremely earthy and astrally inclined. In her DJ sets, she navigates with harmonies through a music selection that combines experimental house from the US Midwest, West African polyrhythms, and music in the tradition of Can from Cologne. „DJ Viola Klein’s R’nB-infused house cuts had the crowd swooning.“ Crack Magazine
slots.name: Viola Klein

slots.bio:
To K15, music is about expression and challenge, a space where fun meets friction and an opportunity to make music from living, not a living from music. His ability to craft music has seen him cultivate a loyal cohort of listeners and made him well respected among peers. He's DJ’d and made music for over 20 years and continues to create - but still wouldn't consider himself a musician.

He has collaborated on various projects with Simbad, Kuniyuki, Mr.G, Earl Jeffers and with Emeson as part of Profusion, as well as producing and remixing music for Children of Zeus, Nubya Garcia and Yelfris Valdés His most recent release is a collaborative EP with Detroit’s finest Kyle Hall. Whether he's K15 or Culross Close, the approach is always the same - make music that's honest of the time.
slots.name: K15

slots.bio: late is of great passion for club dancing and music of many styles. playing records for the dance, he frames a house with windows wide open to much & any other style on the beat, from all corners and many niches of space and time, mixing in balance and melting it all down into one jam that is of strength & spice, that might be of surprise, that draws a four onto a floor where ♥︎ is always on the one
slots.name: late

floors.subtitle: Bring Your Ass at Open Ground

floors.name: LOBBY + ANNEX
floors.subtitle: Drinks and DJ selections free entry

detail.description:
Born out of community driven events in studios and shaped by a strong sense of scene identity, a small group of like minded individuals formed around recaved.collective, united by a shared passion for electronic music. While remaining open to other genres, Recaved dedicates this night entirely to the more serious facets of the sound. No trends, just pure
techno
.

PRUDE is a Wuppertal-based DJ and producer collective. Formed by DJ Frank, Maruhni and paaradoxx, PRUDE connects club culture with community work through events and workshops.

This time they are back at Annex, bringing Brussel based LAURAVIOLI with them for a night full of energy driven curve balls.

Admission 20 € / before 23:00 15 €

slots.bio:
Born in Norilsk, raised between Ukraine and Germany, now based in Dortmund.

The approach is simple: read the room, don’t force a concept. Dychromatic´s DJ sets move between techno, house, minimal, electro, breaks, and trance.

Rooted in DIY and off grid environments, he can usually be found at the edges of the night, beeing a resident DJ at Tresor.West.
slots.name: Dychromatic

slots.bio: Nørbak’s crucial role in today’s techno environment derives from a strong sense of commitment and hard working attitude which he guides towards his musical passion. The Portuguese artist makes use of a thorough knowledge of classic techno and fuses it with modern sound design, the result of which is a signature sound that has seen worldwide support from tier-1 artists and mammoth labels such as Soma Records, Warm Up Recordings and Mord.
slots.name: Nørbak (live)

slots.bio:
Based in Bochum, his connection to electronic music began early, around the age of 12, shaped by the raw energy of drum & bass and dubstep.

For a period, his attention shifted toward hip hop, where he started to lay down an important foundation, influencing the way he approaches rhythm and space in his music today.

Returning to electronic music with a clearer direction, he moved into techno with a focus on dance floor functionality. Over time, that perspective has evolved into a more restrained and deeper sound. His current work is rooted in detailed sound design, with an emphasis on low-end movement.

Rather than creating purely functional club tools, his tracks aim to balance usability with depth—music that works within a dance floor context but also invites a more attentive listening experience

Born into a family of musicians and artists, Steuert's path led from classical piano to techno – a genre that revealed itself as his natural form of expression, further deepened through his time with the Open Ground Crew and their profound understanding of club culture. His approach to DJing resembles storytelling through rhythm, creating moments in which the dancefloor connects with something primal. Although techno forms the centre of his sound, he draws from a wide range of genres to transform individual dancers into a collectively pulsating unity, moving under the spell of hypnotic beats – always in search of the ancient instinct of collective movement.
slots.name: Loris Erdmann b2b Steuert

slots.bio:
DJ Frank doesn’t do boxes. While the Wuppertal based DJ and producer has carried this philosophy throughout her entire life, weaving it into her art is what is now making her an exciting face in the electronic music landscape. With a sound as deep as it is driving and as carefully curated as it is playful, she proves that it’s not just contrast that makes her music so captivating. Exploring the emotional spectrum of electronic music, she blends groovy percussion, textured synth work, and emotional depth into a sound made to move – but also to feel.
​Refining her craft in such a meticulous way over time has also put her in touch with her vision for nightlife: a barrier-free celebration of talent and creativity, which she furthers as a founder of the collective Prude, supplying and curating events & workshops to make space for upcoming talent.
slots.name: DJ Frank

slots.bio:
Proud mom of Who's That Girl? and co-mom of Drum N Babes concepts based in Liège, LAURAVIOLI brings her dynamic, boundary-pushing vision into the local club scene. Seamlessly weaving together UKG, bass music, drum n bass and Latin-infused rhythms, her sets are crafted for pure dancefloor energy, with a sprinkle of edits and guilty pleasures.

Her uncompromising dj style and ability to fuse diverse sounds make her a standout, and she broadens her sonic exploration with a bi-monthly residency on Gimic, a fresh Brussels radio station, where she dives into eclectic electronic sounds that might not typically find their way to the club.
slots.name: LAURAVIOLI

slots.bio:
Maruhni is Wuppertal based DJ, Producer and co-founder of prude collective. together they give dj workshops, host parties and produce music. as an artist, maruhni's music is hugely influenced by different Bass music genres, like Acid, Dub, Jungle or Trance.

paaradoxx (she/her) is a Cologne-based DJ originally from Wuppertal, where she built a strong following as part of the Mauke club. As co-founder of the PRUDE collective, she hosts workshops, a radio show, and throws parties across Germany. She is also a resident DJ at Gewölbe, spinning a wide spectrum of UK sounds, including Grime, UK Garage, Speedgarage, and DnB. Consider this a warning: paaradoxx is a FLINTA* Flummi on a mission. She's here to disrupt, amplify, and reshape the music scene into a truly equitable space, one bouncy beat at a time. So buckle up, or get bounced.
slots.name: Maruhni b2b Paaradoxx

floors.name: LOBBY + ANNEX
floors.subtitle: Drinks and DJ selections free entry

detail.description: Admission 20 € / before 23:00 15 €

slots.bio:
Nada Formosa and C.A. Ramirez are Cologne DJs made by and for the dancefloor, shaped early on by disco and house in all their facets and united by a lifelong pursuit of the groove. Genre-fluid and deeply sound-driven, they blend euphoric highs with deeper, driving passages into a continuous flow of warm energy.

Together, they fill the room with their love for music—creating a vibrant, soulful journey that becomes an inevitable invitation to connection, ecstasy, and collective release. All power to the dancefloor!

Nada Formosa and C.A. Ramirez are Cologne DJs made by and for the dancefloor, shaped early on by disco and house in all their facets and united by a lifelong pursuit of the groove. Genre-fluid and deeply sound-driven, they blend euphoric highs with deeper, driving passages into a continuous flow of warm energy.

Together, they fill the room with their love for music—creating a vibrant, soulful journey that becomes an inevitable invitation to connection, ecstasy, and collective release. All power to the dancefloor!
slots.name: C.A.Ramirez b2b Nada Formosa

slots.bio: There’s a humble power about Kai Alce (pronounced al-SAY). Perhaps it’s a combination of his Haitian roots, his youth spent in St. Croix, and his New York City upbringing. It is evident when he steps behind the turntables or CDJs, where he is at once clearly comfortable and believe it or not, just a tad bit nervous and after more than 20 years in the business, yes; “The Kaizer” still gets butterflies. That’s probably what makes Kai Alce (pronounced al-SAY) such a force: He doesn’t settle down, blend in or go along. That integrity comes through in every gig he’s worked – from his early days at Detroit’s Music Institute when he was just teenager, to the 10-year-long DEEP legacy at MJQ that he co-founded, to the production company, NDATL, that he created in 2008. What’s good is that, just like always, Alce is ready to put in work – staying true to house music, DJing for DJing’s sake, pushing himself … and us on the dance floor. Whether it’s after a night of intense dancing at the club, a session in the booth or studio, or any other creative endeavor, as long as it’s done with truth and sincerity.
slots.name: Kai Alcé

slots.bio: Wuppertal-based DJ duo FKK & Van der Bolt met nine years ago and bonded over a shared love for design, music and club culture. As Kiosk Force, they are known for their playful style and have developed a groovy electronic rooted sound with raw club vibes and danceable rhythms – often driven by acid-influenced basslines. Their sets mean to captivate and ground listeners in the present moment.
slots.name: Kioskforce

slots.bio:
Based in the Ruhr Area and with a residency at Tresor.West, medea navigates the deeper ends of techno with a focus on texture and control.

Her sound is rooted in rolling, dub-leaning structures and atmospheric layering, occasionally tracing back to Detroit without leaning on nostalgia. Sets unfold patiently, favouring continuity over impact, and are shaped with a strong awareness for space, timing and system response.
slots.name: medea

slots.bio:
Kyohara and ÆKO, founders of Kontur, create hypnosis through repetition and subtle movement. With sharing the same perspective on sound their live sets are built on improvisation, reacting to the moment rather than following a fixed plan. Each performance unfolds naturally, shaped by the space and the energy in the room.

Kyohara and ÆKO, founders of Kontur, create hypnosis through repetition and subtle movement. With sharing the same perspective on sound their live sets are built on improvisation, reacting to the moment rather than following a fixed plan. Each performance unfolds naturally, shaped by the space and the energy in the room.
slots.name: ÆKO b2b Kyohara

slots.bio:
Balance within contrasts.
She approaches art through the belief that balance can only emerge from opposition — harmony is born through dynamics. A dialogue between softness and force, control and surrender — a dance of tension and release.
Guided by psychoacoustic intuition, she sculpts textures that blur the edge of consciousness. The low end anchors, the highs cut, the mids breathe. Rolling drums create a constant motion — a rhythmic ground that nourishes the magnetic midrange elements shaping the narrative.
What connects it all is the pursuit of perpetual balance: a continuous loop between pressure and relief, clarity and blur, chaos and control — a living tension that never fully resolves, yet keeps the body in motion and the mind awake.
slots.name: Mara Menace

floors.name: LOBBY + ANNEX
floors.subtitle: Drinks and DJ selections free entry

detail.description: Admission 20 € / before 23:00 15 €

slots.bio: The Tottenham London born-and-bred selector grew up on soul and reggae, later becoming a dedicated junglist. First as a resident DJ at the legendary London club Plastic People, she’s built a rep as one of the most effective and interesting DJs around. Playing shape-shifting, genre-bending sets that effortlessly move from classic rare groove and disco records to upfront house and techno, via garage, jungle and whatever else she feels like. With no social media presence, Josey’s reputation is based on selection, passion and authenticity.
slots.name: Josey Rebelle

slots.bio: Kode9 is a DJ/Producer, artist and writer. He set up the record label Hyperdub in 2004, and in 2019 the sub label Flatlines to focus on audio essays and sonic fiction.From 2017, he co-curated Hyperdub’s monthly event series Ø. He has recorded 4 albums, 2 with the late vocalist The Spaceape; Memories of Future (2006) and Black Sun (2010), and 2 solo albums, Nothing (2015) and Escapology (2022). He has compiled 4 DJ mix compilations, Dubstep Allstars vol.3 (Tempa 2006), DJ Kicks (K7 2011) and Rinse 22 (Rinse 2013), and in 2018, Fabric 100 with Burial. In additional to his record label Hyperdub, he has also released tracks on Aphex Twin’s Rephlex label, Soul Jazz and Rinse and remixed for Mr. Fingers, Lee Scratch Perry, the Junior Boys, Battles and Dabrye/MF Doom among others.
slots.name: Kode9

slots.bio:
Using the structures of music as mediums in her multi-media universe, Juliana Huxtable is a DJ and musician singular in her approach. Where her visual art and poetry navigate the complexification of desire in a life increasingly mediated by technology, her music utilizes the sounds of technology itself to construct parallel realities to be inhabited and embodied in rhythm and harmonic tableaux.

Her sets skillfully deploy the notion of sampling and re-blogging as DJ strategies, ecstatically mixing an array of influences that frolic at the boundary of genre-intuition and experimentation. At once an assertion of freedom and an ode to the evolutionary structure of electronic music subcultures, she aspires to the sublime in what can often only be described as witchcraft behind the decks; seance from the stage.
slots.name: J​uliana Huxtable

slots.bio:
Christian Coiffure is an enigmatic artist steadily making his mark on the European electronic scene, with standout releases on the adventurous labels Comic Sans Records and Worst Records, as well as remixes for internationally renowned artists such as Tatyana Jane and Anastasia Kristensen.

A member of the useless seconds label and collective, he refuses to be defined by any single genre, playfully blending styles and tempos while shaping a sound that fuses forward-thinking design, twisted club motifs, and driving grooves. Drawn from years of deep listening and analysis of electronic music, he aspires to shape the sonic landscape of tomorrow by bending and reinterpreting the aesthetics that inspire him.

Christian Coiffure is devoted to refining his craft — both as a DJ and live performer, delivering captivating, transportive sets, and as a producer, where his inventiveness and attention to detail resonate strongly with his listeners.
slots.name: C. Coiffure

slots.bio: With a fascination for all things bass & breaks, matildnz likes to play whatever tickles her brain, touches her soul or gets her body moving — preferably all at once. Aiming to create more spaces for unique electronic sounds, the DJ, designer and digital artist has been involved in the local music scene for quite some time now, organizing events as part of IVORY as well as curating the online radio station beton.fm.
slots.name: Matildnz

slots.bio:
Wuppertal-based artist and head of the Ballett label – crafts immersive, deep-textured soundscapes that blur the lines between dub-infused techno and ambient minimalism. His sets are shaped by warm, grainy rhythms, atmospheric grooves, and an earthy sonic palette that feels both grounded and expansive.

Rooted in classic dub aesthetics yet forward-thinking in execution, Mauzius merges analog warmth with precise electronic detail. The result is a sound that is organically fluid yet tightly structured — creating intimate moments between sound and movement.

Whether in the studio or behind the decks, Mauzius curates a dynamic and deeply engaging journey, marked by subtle shifts, hypnotic patterns, and a tactile sense of depth.
slots.name: Mauzius

detail.description: Admission 20 € / before 23:00 15 €

slots.bio:
DJ Frog was born, raised, and taught-to-groove in Montreal. Her preference is for the chuggy, chunky, dusty, and loopy ends of house and techno.
She’s in a love affair with the FM compressor at CKUT. DJ Frog also makes music and throws parties with her friends at Doo / Greenhouse.
slots.name: DJ Frog

slots.bio:
XDB is Kosta Athanassiadis – German by birth and in residence, with Greek parentage. His roots are in the central German town of Göttingen. It was here that he began DJing in the early nineties; where he worked in a record shop; where he ran a radio show for five years; and the base from which he established the Metrolux record label in 2006.
Kosta’s DJ sets draw upon his expansive record collection, playing right across the board of the quality side of electronic music. He has no shortage of technical skill and prefers a 3 decks setup, but despite his mixing skills it’s his selection that truly shines – putting the music itself at the forefront, letting songs breathe when need be, and delivering plenty of head-scratching ‘what the hell is this track?’ moments.
slots.name: XDB

slots.bio:
V.Dietz is a chilean DJ and selector currently based in Düsseldorf.

Influenced by the minimal house scene from Chile and since then evolving into the exploration of electro, acid house, italo and everything in between.

The philosophy is simple: good music is good music. His selections are united by curiosity, emotion and a commitment to quality sound.
slots.name: V.Dietz

slots.bio: Cynthia Matisse has always been driven by a passion for vinyl DJing. As a resident at Triebwerk Erfurt she explores a wide spectrum of electronic music – from Techno and House to Bass, Dubstep, DnB. From warm, groovy tunes to rousing, hypnotic atmospheres, or functional, energetic and driving sounds. Her aim is to always take the audience on an unpredictable journey with a heartfully thought out landscape. In her selection she has a very emotional approach to music, focused on creating depth and a captivating energy.
slots.name: Cynthia Matisse
Flotze

detail.description: Admission 40 €

slots.bio:
Sadar Bahar didn’t become a DJ he grew into one. Raised in Chicago when the city was still a living laboratory for house, disco and late-night radio experimentation, he absorbed music as culture before it was industry. Long before international flights and global bookings, there were radio tapes, record stores, basement parties, and an obsession with understanding why certain grooves hit deeper than others.
​His education didn’t come from charts. It came from dance floors. From the raw energy of early Chicago, from DJs who played with fearless abandon, from a time when the booth wasn’t a stage but a command center. Figures like Ron Hardy weren’t references to name-drop, they were part of the atmosphere that shaped his ear. That foundation built a simple but uncompromising philosophy: music must move the body before it feeds the ego.

A relentless digger and lifelong vinyl collector, Sadar treats record hunting as daily practice. He doesn’t chase rarity for status; he searches for tension, soul, rhythm, and emotional weight. His sets glide through obscure disco, early house, leftfield soul and raw electronics without ever sounding nostalgic.

His long-running party Soul In The Hole reflects that same ethos: a space where music is physical, communal and uncompromised. The dance floor comes first.

Technology has evolved. Club culture has shifted. His core hasn’t. Playing vinyl isn’t a retro gesture it’s the format that mirrors his approach: tactile, imperfect, alive. For Sadar, the DJ isn’t a performer chasing attention, but a conduit controlling energy in real time.
slots.name: Sadar Bahar

slots.bio: Theo Parrish is a Washington D.C.-born, Chicago-raised DJ, producer, selector, arranger, keyboard clunker, machine beater, writer, sculptor, car lover, visual artist, lecturer, and father that loves creativity. Detroit’s creative community has recognized his input since 1995, the era of Buy Rite Records, Saint Andrew’s Hall, the Rhythm Kitchen, and Alvin’s. He started Sound Signature Recordings, which has manufactured vinyl records independently from 1997 to this day — exclusively using the legendary Archer Record Pressing Co. on Davison Avenue. He continues to engage, challenge, and redefine the limits of what we call Black dance music, serving as an example of creative independence. Parrish holds a BFA in sound sculpture from Kansas City Art Institute.
slots.name: Theo Parrish

floors.name: ANNEX

slots.bio: JASSS is a Spanish sound artist, producer, and DJ with releases on labels such as AD-93, Ostgut Ton, and Never Sleep, aside from a residency in Herrensauna. Her retrofuturist style foregrounds bass and percussion, approaching but denying a techno orientation by embracing atmospheres of industrial pop, nu-metal distortion, and fuzzed-out R&B. Her sound is always rooted in full-flavour, high-impact stuff that is brilliantly compelling and strobe lit throughout, whether stepping at half time or falling over itself in a race to the end.
slots.name: JASSS

slots.bio:
Joe Cotch is a music curator, researcher, A&R, DJ and producer. Starting his journey as club promoter and DJ in Granada, Spain in his late teens, Joe moved back to his birthplace of London, ultimately playing a key role at Peckham-based record shop / night club / community hub Rye Wax. Alongside that, he co-founded and A&R’d the Cotch International record label and event series, which has been a key part of London nightlife for over 10 years, showcasing and platforming emerging talent from all corners of the globe.

A dedicated digger and student of global soundsystem culture, his polyrhythm-heavy sets stretch across all BPMs, joining the dots between interconnected global subcultures, united by a sense of urgency and visceral club beats, and served up through a distinctly LDN 'pirate radio' lens. This outlook is displayed on his monthly NTS Radio show 'Platos Combinados', which has included guests as varied as Nick Leon, DJ Lag and Simo Cell, to Moonchild Sanelly, Novelist and Rennan Da Penha. Alongside this, he has continued his A&R work, co-curating the 'Amapiano Now' compilation for NTS Records, and curating South African artists for the recent 'Black Panther: Wakanda Forever Soundtrack', amongst many other projects.
slots.name: Joe Cotch

floors.subtitle: Cotch International presents at Open Ground

detail.description: Admission 20 € / before 23:00 15 €

slots.bio: Kangding Ray is the alias of musician and producer David Letellier, whose work fuses experimentalism and club music. Widely recognized as one of the most innovative voices in contemporary electronic music, he explores the emotional and physical dimensions of sound — from immersive live sets to award-winning film scores and conceptual sound design.
slots.name: Kangding Ray

slots.bio: Gerard Hanson was born in 1972. He hails from of Dallas, Texas USA. Little is known about this producer who seems to have Scandinavian roots. His productions have reflections with Basic Channel/Chain Reaction sound. His main inspirations are Juan Atkins and Derrick May. His music also has influences from Stefan Robbers, Red Planet and Carl Craig. He found a way where everything came together: Deepness, Minimalism and Soul, the Detroit-Berlin connection. In 1994 Gerard Hanson and Dan Kurzius started ‘Hardsync’, a Magazine dedicated to electronic music reviews and artist interviews. It has been defunct since 1996 as a result of a computer crash. Hard Sync was an outlet for several Texas-based electronic music enthusiasts to reach out to others around the world that shared similar musical interests. He released his first 12″ on Matrix in 1995. From 1995-1997, he released five ep’s, and then only two more in the next six years until 2005. After that some other labels approached him but just wanted more of the same and didn’t really seem to understand his sound. After that he stopped releasing music and produced only for his personal usage. After a couple of years the label Down Low convinced him to pick up his producing again. He is very careful about sharing his music with the rest of the world. In the last decade he only used the aliases Convextion and E.R.P. (Event Related Potential). E.R.P is an electro-edged moniker. Gerard has remained willingly anonymous since his debut in 1995, but he came out of obscurity for a debut European Live performance in London in April 2006.
slots.name: Convextion (live)

slots.bio:
GiGi FM’s selections have charmed bodies on dancefloors across Europe and beyond into states of ecstasy. Techno, bass, breaks and ambient all come to play when she’s in the booth.
Propelling her audiences from carnal, bass-driven depths to a heady ambient trance in a matter of minutes, she commands the floor with her genre-defying spirit.
slots.name: GiGi FM

slots.bio: The man behind the label funkscapes is releasing fresh grooves across different electronic genres as well as reissuing forgotten disco and boogie funk gems. As a DJ he's blending those 1980s joints with timeless and current house, techno, acid and electro flavors. Musical richness and unexpected turns are guaranteed.
slots.name: Benedikt Meger

slots.bio:
Eoism is a duo from Leipzig and Jena, devoted to electro and Detroit-influenced techno. They are producing, deejaying and playing live on their Electribes, as well as running their label Pulse Drift Recordings since 2016. They are also organizing parties in their area and keep the electro scene alive.

Beside releasing on their own imprint they released on Undersound Recordings, Inch By Inch Records, funkscapes, mindcolormusic and Sounds Benefit among others. By the end of last year they dropped the 'Cellarworx II' EP and their live-set at Golden Pudel Club in Hamburg set the club on fire. Look forward to their live-set at Open Ground!
slots.name: Eoism (live)

slots.bio:
For more than a decade Alexander Dorn has shaken electronic sounds, stirring shapes and forms as Credit 00 that adhere to few genre tags.
Being sonically socialized through hip hop in the 1990s, graffiti and breakdance were the first outlets for his creativity, music dominating his daily life since a young age. It was through hip hop culture that he realised that you don't need a degree or know how to play an instrument to create your own music.

Inspired by the US ghetto music of the 1980s his journey to explore the endless universe of sound had kicked off. Leading him through many phases of musical preferences: classic electro, reggae dancehall, italo disco, Chicago house, Detroit techno, new wave or ghettotech.

After concentrating on numerous styles his mission became to combine different influences in DJing and production. Music without genre and boundaries is also the concept of his label Rat Life Records. Since 2014 it is a platform for his own output and those of like-minded artists.

Having released many EPs, compilations and collaborations on labels like Uncanny Valley, Chiwax, June, Bordello A Parigi, Pinkman, especially his 2017 debut album 'Game Over' shows the many voices of an open minded artist. Even now Credit 00 is always on the lookout for the unknown and seeks to challenge the listener and himself rather than to fulfill expectations.
For funkscapes he just contributed a fresh rework to the extended reissue of Pop Projekt's 'Elektrik Boogie'.
slots.name: Credit 00

detail.description: Admission 20 € / before 23:00 15 €

slots.bio:
Erika dreams on the cellular level, or perhaps of transdimensional intelligences moving through strange patterns on celestial objects, working towards a mysterious goal. Her connection to the dreamworld becomes concrete in her approach to music, simultaneously so solid and yet so ethereal.

Erika began performing live in Ectomorph in 1997 when she was gifted a TR-606 by BMG and asked to join the group. This grew to her building her own studio, performing solo as Erika, collaborating with people like Jay Ahern and Noncompliant, and performing as a member of Circle of Live. Her depth of thought and clarity of vision has led to her mentoring people on live performance through the In Bloom platform, where she has made a large impact on many up and coming musicians.
slots.name: Erika

slots.bio: Brendan M Gillen is the founder of cult label Interdimensional Transmissions, central figure in Ectomorph, and legendary DJ and live act under his moniker BMG. His multi decade musical vision and near encyclopedic musical knowledge have led him to be one of the key figures and visionaries in contemporary underground dance music. His parties (most famously but not limited to No Way Back and Return to the Source) are the greatest contemporary manifestation of the Detroit party continuum that reaches back to the parties that formed Detroit’s underground roots.
slots.name: BMG

slots.bio: Influenced by early Detroit techno and Berlin dub techno, drum'n'bass and IDM, the Berghain resident likes to break the frame as a producer & DJ. Coupled with fast and razor-sharp multi-deck mixing, he continues to develop his DJ sets with forward thrust and pursues the goal of a unity-creating experience on the floor. His productions and sets are united by a constant lookout for groove, flow, reduction and repetition and follow the urge to surrender oneself solely to the music.
slots.name: Fadi Mohem

slots.bio: Paris-born and Cologne-based DJ and curator Sharlie explores a wide range of musical genres within a boundless sonic unity. Her sound is defined by an organic assemblage of industrial textures, acid elements, and concrete basslines—all moving in a constant orbit between all forms of house, amapiano, Afro Tech, world music, and more.
slots.name: Sharlipseon

slots.bio:
Neue Grafik is a House, Jazz and Hip-Hop DJ and producer.

With only 3 records behind him on esteemed labels like Beat X Changers and S3A Records, producer and DJ Fred Bwelle aka Neue Grafik has quickly risen as one of the most promising acts to come out of the new French and European electronic scene (ask Laurent Garnier, he will confirm). His sound is best described as eclectic and versatile. It’s the product of a mixture of roots and a wide-ranging palette of inspiration. In his music you can hear the sound of Paris (his home-town), its music and cultural legacy, the ethnic sounds of Africa, as well as House, Jazz, Broken Beat and Hip-Hop. Go check him out playing live on his MPCs, you’ll witness what the man is all about.
slots.name: Neue Grafik (live)

slots.bio:
E.P.I.Q. is an eight-member DJ crew from Cologne who have made it their mission to bring their individual sounds together for musical journeys through the night. They collaborate with other DJs, artists, and creatives and organize events on the local and national scene.

Come to their dance floor and let yourself be infected and carried away—an energy that cannot be explained, only experienced. E.P.I.Q.'s goal is to create a space characterized by mutual support where they can play and share their favorite music and convey a sense of community and cohesion.

Tonight we present a a smaller group within the E.P.I.Q. collective, consisting of Anna Cainelli and Nikity. From soul to hip-hop, R&B to world pop, house to breakbeats, bass to UK garage, and more, they provide cute B2B2Bs, loving energy, and booty-shaking all night long.
slots.name: Anna Cainelli & Nikity

slots.bio: DJ Ass Tits is part of @hyperlovecollective and co-founder of the queer music festival @koelnchella. For some time now, Hyperlovecollective has had a residency at Cologne's Jaki Club. They are using this as an opportunity to give queer flinta* artists this stage and create a club night where all participants can feel comfortable. Since April 2026, DJ ASS TITS is co-hosting the show “i hope this finds you well” on @dublab.de, where she expresses her love of ambient music together with paaradoxx and their guests. DJ ASS TITS' sound crosses genres, moving predominantly within a spectrum between breakbeat, electro, acid house, and techno. But it doesn't matter, as long as it gets your a$$es $haking.
slots.name: DJ Ass Tits

detail.description: Admission 20 € / before 23:00 15 €

slots.bio: Beste Hira is an Istanbul-born, Amsterdam-based artist operating at the intersection of rolling, minimal techno and experimental sound. Classically trained, her sets are marked by a refined sense of structure, texture and control; where she moves fluidly between groove-led rhythms and ambient abstraction. A regular at Berghain since 2022 and a key fixture within the Amsterdam scene, she has steadily established herself as a distinctive and uncompromising voice within the contemporary underground.
slots.name: Beste Hira

slots.bio: Toobris grew up in Munich and later moved to Stuttgart for studies, also spending time in Berlin and Amsterdam. The vibrant musical scenes in these cities played a key role in shaping his sound. Known for embracing randomness, his tracks feature unpredictable elements that come together cohesively when heard as a whole. Toobris’ music is soulful, groovy, and bouncy, with basslines that have rhythmic quality that invites movement, complemented by crisp hi-hats and rich percussive textures. With a minimalist approach and a focus on sound design, Toobris creates a unique sonic landscape that feels both intricate, captivating and sure to make you dance.
slots.name: Toobris

slots.bio:
Freddy K: techno activist. Since the ‘90s Alessio Armeni has been tireless campaigning from the dancefloor, defending vinyl, championing young talents and reinforcing the scene from the shadows. You will know him best for his marathon DJ sets and deviant selections.
Freddy K sets are characteristically mercurial and 100% tailored for the dance. He plays a “360°-style of techno,” which can include Soft Cell, WestBam or Bronski Beat—when the timing is just right.
slots.name: Freddy K

detail.description: Admission 30 €

slots.bio: Making waves now for over 2 decades, SP:MC has cemented himself as one of the most respected & busiest MCs on the circuit. Versatility & quality control are guaranteed when SP’s in the building. He cites MCs GQ, Dynamite & Conrad as early influences. SP was crowned ‘Best MC’ at the D&BArena Awards in 2011.
slots.name: SP:MC (DJ set)

slots.bio:
Belgian born Alix Depauw is the face behind production moniker Alix Perez and collaborative duo SHADES (w/ Eprom). Alix released his debut album "1984" to critical acclaim in 2009 which is still talked about now as one of the most accomplished Drum and Bass albums of all time.
Fast forward to 2025, his record label 1985 Music stands as one of the most forward thinking platforms in electronic music, host to a number of ground breaking artists and revered releases.
Alix has worked with some of the foremost names in dance and electronic music including; Noisia, Foreign Beggars, MachineDrum and many more. Following the release of his 2nd album Chroma Chords in 2013, he delivered a BBC Radio 1 Essential Mix, which was shortlisted for essential mix of the year.
In 2016 Alix launched his own imprint; 1985 Music. Now in its 9th year since inception, the label is
regarded as one of the most tasteful audio & visual platforms in the electronic realm. 1985 Music promotes
multiple club nights globally.
​Alix’s high levels of creativity and discipline are the reason he is very much at the forefront of electronic music today. His focus always remains in the art and the music by sticking true to his
roots and vision.. He is a true artist and a trailblazer in his own right.
slots.name: Alix Perez

slots.bio: Tasha’s DJing style weaves tunes together seamlessly from her bass informed home of the UK alongside four four techno forms born in Berlin and Detroit. With an accomplished and atmospheric hold on the dancefloor expect upfront cuts mixed in with some old gems and other styles in between like D&B / Jungle. In an industry where artists are asked to prioritize their art for social media presence, likes and other vacuous pursuits, Tasha is very much in demand for her ‘music first’ attitude, selection and ability.
slots.name: Tasha

slots.bio: Paulita is a young selector with an old soul, known for bringing an infectious, high-vibrational energy and a raw, physical presence to the booth. Born in LA, shaped by her Chilean heritage, and now based in Berlin, she is a DJ who leads by example—dancing as hard as the front row to a deep-dug blend of Brazilian gems, deep-cut funk, raw disco, and slow-burning soulful house. For Paulita, the dance floor is a space of freedom and legacy. With a decade of record collecting behind her, she specializes in finding the "real music"—the soulful, unexpected tracks you never knew you needed. This commitment to sound extends beyond the booth through her work with independent labels and her own creative outlets, CACTUS and Sobremesa Sounds. Whether she’s headlining an underground room or a sun-drenched terrace, Paulita strips away the pressure of the "boom" to bring the soul, the sweat, and a genuine human connection back to the center of the room.
slots.name: Paulita

slots.bio:
Craig White’s love affair with house music began in the early 2000s, inspired by the sounds of New York. Over a decade ago he launched his career with a four-year London residency, playing venues like Number 90 Hackney, The Cause, and 93 Feet East. Since relocating to Berlin in 2018, he has become a fixture of the city’s underground scene with sets at Paloma, Sisyphos, and Renate, alongside appearances in Amsterdam (RadioRadio, Garage Noord) and in-store sessions at Rush Hour Amsterdam and BBE London.

Craig’s sets weave a rich tapestry of grooves from true masters of the craft, allowing him to build journeys, create moments, and let the music breathe as intended.

He is also the driving force behind Mister Bear Records—a Berlin-based party of seven years and a label two years strong—featuring releases from Jimpster, Kai Alcé, Vick Lavender, and Alton Miller. With editions in Berlin, London, Amsterdam, and New York, Mister Bear has grown into an internationally recognized event.

Whether behind the decks or through his label, Craig stays true to the deep, raw essence of house music.
slots.name: Craig White

slots.bio:
The son of Colombian and Mexican immigrants, Zapata was born in New York, and grew up on a diet of salsa, jazz, rock, and hip-hop. After playing jazz trombone when he was young, he began DJing after falling in love with the kind of jazz-infused house that much of 90’s New York was all about. He discovered deeper sounds (from Detroit, Chicago, and back to NYC) the more he dug, moving through a lot of the dreamier, chordier house that defines much of his musical output. You can expect anything from disco, deep, salsa, afro-house, hip-house, hip-hop, and tons of jams with live elements when he plays DJ sets.

Zapata is all about diversity and genuineness. This outlook can be seen when he spins at DiscoDisco, a party which he co-founded, alongside Nils Nilsson and Agha B, at Berlin’s underground Repeat Bar—a monthly get-down which encourages DJs to break the four-to-the-floor mould by playing funk, jazz, afro, salsa, and anything else that falls in between. Music always stands the test of time—that’s why it’s important to really believe in the records you play. There should be no such thing as fillers, he believes. Play only what you really love, and be real with it!
slots.name: Zapata

detail.description: Admission 20 € / before 23:00 15 €

slots.bio:
The British-born, Berlin-based artist Sam Barker has long sought to push the boundaries of electronic music, in and out of the club. Looking beyond the realm of 4/4 techno to explore left-field sound design and rhythmic complexity, the Berghain resident’s string of signature solo releases for the club’s Ostgut Ton imprint and Smalltown Supersound – including Debiasing, Utility, and 2025's Stochastic Drift – have earned widespread acclaim from the underground to the mainstream and everything in between.

Barker’s musical focus continues to be on the peak-time dancefloor potential of ambient music as he works on another album and label. Operating on the periphery of club music, he continues to shift its centre.
slots.name: Barker

slots.bio:
Rene Wise’s approach to writing music and DJing fuses together as one concept; tapping into primal human instinct via rhythm and groove.
Wise portrays a deep sense of knowledge and maturity in his sound, devising atmospheric soundscapes with a raw but modern touch, captivating the listener from beginning to end. His sets demonstrate this to full effect, consistently maintaining a powerful sonic trademark whilst possessing the innate ability to create hip-shaking poly-rhythms and multi-deck layers.
slots.name: Rene Wise

detail.description: Admission 20 € / before 23:00 15 €

slots.bio:
Errol is one of the UK's most compelling, contemporary musical voices: DJ, NTS Radio staple, cultural curator, community builder and founder of forward-thinking musical movement, Touching Bass.

As a DJ, he's known for his beautifully loose, searching and soulful sets; unrestricted by genre and anchored by groove. Stepping into 2026 with a blossoming reputation for fostering thoughtful, soulful experiences; both on the dancefloor and across world-renowned cultural spaces. From residencies at Corsica Studios/The Carpet Shop, moments at Open Ground, Panorama Bar, Nowadays and Public Records to his curatorial prowess at the Barbican Centre (an ongoing residency), Frieze Week, Lisson Gallery and Le Guess Who? Festival to name a few.

All this while Touching Bass — the London-based musical movement encompassing an award-nominated record label, club night, concert series, staple NTS Radio show and curatorial studio — continues to slow cook. Internationally known for its dedication to tastemaking musical eclecticism, intention and community spirit.
slots.name: Errol

slots.bio: The Tottenham London born-and-bred selector grew up on soul and reggae, later becoming a dedicated junglist. First as a resident DJ at the legendary London club Plastic People, she’s built a rep as one of the most effective and interesting DJs around. Playing shape-shifting, genre-bending sets that effortlessly move from classic rare groove and disco records to upfront house and techno, via garage, jungle and whatever else she feels like. With no social media presence, Josey’s reputation is based on selection, passion and authenticity.
slots.name: Josey Rebelle

slots.bio: Mali Larrington-Nelson AKA Shy One lives and breathes the eclecticism and diversity of her DJ sets and productions. Having started off DJing on pirate radio as a teenager, Shy One has been exposed to and influenced by the full spectrum of sounds her hometown of London has birthed and nurtured over the years. As with many young Londoners at the time, grime and the wider family of indigenous London club music was what gave Shy One the passion to pursue music as more than a pastime. Obsessively collecting white labels by some of her local production heroes quickly developed into obsessively making beats in Fruity Loops for a handful of local MCs bubbling up in Harrow, the North West London borough she grew up in. Undeterred by those in the scene who called her beats “weird” or “different”, Shy One ploughed on carving out her own sound which eventually caught the ear of Scratcha DVA, who went on to release the Decaffeinated Love EP and her debut LP, Bedknobs and Boomkicks, on his now renowned DVA Music imprint.
slots.name: Shy One

slots.bio: Viola Klein’s records are supremely earthy and astrally inclined. In her DJ sets, she navigates with harmonies through a music selection that combines experimental house from the US Midwest, West African polyrhythms, and music in the tradition of Can from Cologne. „DJ Viola Klein’s R’nB-infused house cuts had the crowd swooning.“ Crack Magazine
slots.name: Viola Klein

slots.bio: As Wupperal born native of the 90s, Danyon was not only deeply influenced by his fathers vinyl and electronic music collection, but also the upcoming years of clubbing and DJing from 2013 onwards. His sound most likely be described as percussion-rich, with rumbling base-lines right up to broken beat techno.
slots.name: Danyon

slots.bio: Mexem has been active in Wuppertal’s underground music scene for over a decade. Whether performing in clubs or crafting live sets, his work is defined by a distinctive approach to sound design, groove, and the dynamic tension between melancholy and euphoria. His sets weave together ambient textures, deep dubstep, and intricate shades of jungle and drum & bass—always grounded in his unmistakable sonic identity, with deep, driving techno as the foundation. Mexem’s music draws listeners into a trance-like state and guides them through a rich, transformative journey.
slots.name: Mexem (live)

slots.bio: Rosa, now based in Rotterdam, found creative ways to thrive at night in her former hometown by organizing free outdoor parties and founding the collective Rooibosch. She later started Draaisessies in Tilburg: a community hub offering free music production and DJ lessons to local youth. Rosa’s musical style blends diverse rhythms and genres; focusing on bass-heavy, percussion-driven tracks in club settings, while her radio show Work in Progress on Operator features warm, experimental music for relaxed listening.
slots.name: Rosa

slots.bio:
KØKØ, born and based in Wuppertal, draws inspiration from the atmospheric styles of the 2010s techno scene.

His sound is a fusion of deep, ambient textures and driving beats, influenced by immersive, hypnotic and dynamic elements. His style binds the essence of both introspective soundscapes and raw energy, creating a unique auditory experience that reflects his roots and artistic evolution.
slots.name: Køkø

detail.description: Admission 20 € / before 23:00 15 €

slots.bio:
Luna Ludmila (Utrecht, NL) has spent the past two decades shaping her distinct sound as a DJ and producer. Blending dub-inspired grooves, dusty house records, and intricate tribal rhythms, she crafts hypnotic sonic journeys that captivate dance floors. A staple in the Dutch electronic music scene, Luna has played at renowned clubs and events such as Breakfast Club, Is Burning, VBX, Lofi and De School. Her international presence has taken her to iconic venues like Tresor, Club der Visionaere, Lux Frágil and Macadam. In 2023, she debuted her first-ever live club set at the revered Bassiani, marking a new chapter in her artistic evolution. Since then, she has played live at STOOR at Paradiso, BRET, WAS., and festivals including La Nature (BE) and Drift.

Beyond the dancefloor, Luna Ludmila continues to push artistic boundaries. After earning a degree in composition and sound design at the HKU in 2021, she expanded her focus to ambient/downtempo music, and interdisciplinary collaborations. In November 2024, she premiered her first audiovisual performance with visual artist Grigory Gromov at Podium Victorie in The Netherlands. Another standout moment was her sold out ambient live performance during ZERO BPM at the Thomaskerk in Amsterdam.

While performing is a deep passion, Luna spends most of her time in the studio, exploring the nuances of sound design and rhythmic complexity. Her fascination with layering textures and experimenting with intricate beats has led to releases on esteemed labels such as SoHaSo and Berg Audio. Whether behind the decks, crafting ambient journeys, or pushing boundaries with multidisciplinary performances, Luna Ludmila continues to evolve, creating a sound that is both timeless and forward-thinking.
slots.name: Luna Ludmila

slots.bio:
“Extremely versatile, endlessly curious about music and machines, yet always modest and self-critical.” That’s how friends and fellow artists often describe Steve Rachmad.
Over the years, the Amsterdam producer has created a constellation of aliases to reflect the breadth of his musical ideas. Each name represents a different facet of his sound — a way to explore distinct territories within electronic music. Several of these identities have taken on lives of their own in the DJ booth.

One of the key characters in this universe is Parallel 9. With this alias, Rachmad explores the deeper and more hypnotic edges of electronic music. A Parallel 9 set is a journey through timeless depth — drawing from vintage deep house, dub-infused techno and productions by kindred spirits who share a fascination for the more immersive side of the underground.

Parallel 9 reveals yet another dimension of Rachmad’s musical language: subtle, hypnotic and emotionally rich. Whether it’s a decades-old classic or a new production, the music speaks the same vocabulary —depth, honesty and profound love for the craft.

In 2026, as Steve Rachmad marks 45 years devoted to music, the Parallel 9 alias will appear at selected moments in selected locations. This includes Kynant showcases at Panoramabar in Berlin and Open Ground in Wuppertal, as well as the 9 Years Blitz celebration in Munich.

British-Nigerian DJ and curator Richard Akingbehin is the co-founder of Berlin-based radio station Refuge Worldwide, and the founder of techno, dub and experimental label Kynant Records. His DJ sets move between dub techno,house and ambient, with soundsystem culture as the founding influence.
slots.name: Parallel 9 b2b Richard Akingbehin

slots.bio:
As a DJ, Altinbas is known for Challenging the limits of modern techno. Crafting captivating journeys that explore the common threads and connective tissue between his carefully-selected cuts.
Through his mixes, he’s become known for a sound built on layered beats and rapidly shifting dynamics, where even familiar tracks feel fresh in the fabric of his sonic narratives.
slots.name: Altinbas

slots.bio:
Phonetisch Athletisch startet as a radio session on dublab DE and follows the idea of staying musically open and flexible - somewhere between Disco, Boogie and Funk and whatever feels right at the time.

Chiara is a DJ based in Cologne and part of the dublab team. Drawn to textured, atmospheric grooves, she also enjoys exploring a wide range of musical directions. Her sets are shaped by the mood of a space, translating moments and emotions into sound.

Ruben Nicolas has been part of the dublab.de community for years. With roots in Funk, Disco, Jazz and Hip Hop, his musical perspective has gradually expanded towards late-'90s and early-2000s Electronica, Downtempo and Organic House. Drawn to grooves, textures and timeless rhythms, he enjoys connecting different musical worlds and finding unexpected links between the familiar and the overlooked. His selections move fluidly between warm, soulful moments and deeper electronic excursions, always guided by curiosity and a sense of flow.
slots.name: Prima Gentle Phonetics

slots.bio:
Only a handful of years in, and Mankiyan is already taking Belgium by storm.

A sure favourite of the scene, this powerhouse of the new generation is marking her territory.

Mankiyan’s mixes are full of depth, spatiality and intuition, spanning across heady dub, rave, UK bass and grime.

She toys with complex rhythm sequences and easily switches genres, with healthy splashes of bright twists – but always with an impeccable sense of pace and flow.

Mankiyan is injecting community-driven, unpretentious and unadulterated love for rave culture back into nightlife.
slots.name: Mankiyan

slots.bio:
For over 10 years, Cologne-based C U SOON has been spinning the decks. Before that, he spent years playing in brass ensembles – from orchestras and quintets to solo performances – which led him to try out DJing after his first experiences at electronic parties. Through creating parties and working with different projects in DUS/AC/CGN, he has found diverse styles for different occasions – from ambient and OG house beats to techy electronic drives. Always surprising – mostly delighting.

Florian, aka Ghiro, is a Cologne-based filmmaker, DJ and member of the community radio station dublab.de. Back in 2017, he co-founded the bass music event series Basskombüse in Mönchengladbach — at a time when bass music felt far from the spotlight. With roots in soulful and deep Drum & Bass, his musical language has gradually opened up towards Jungle, Dubstep, Dub Techno, House, and more experimental forms of electronic music. A strong sense of storytelling also shapes his approach to DJing. His sets bring together pulse, warmth, and subtle shifts in energy, balancing organic sounds with unexpected turns and long-form flow.
slots.name: C U SOON b2b Ghiro
